topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the locker room felt thick, suffocating. Leo stared at the roster posted on the corkboard, his heart hammering against his ribs like a trapped bird. He had poured every ounce of his being into those grueling practices, the early mornings, the near-constant pain, all for this moment. His name wasn't there. A single, stark line of black marker crossed out next to his number. A cold knot tightened in his stomach, spreading outwards until his hands started to tremble uncontrollably. He tried to force a casual shrug, but his throat felt constricted, refusing to produce any sound. Every glance from his teammates felt like a spotlight, exposing the gaping hole where his acceptance should have been. He could feel a clammy sweat prickling his palms, and he desperately wanted to bolt, to disappear before anyone noticed the sheer, hollow emptiness radiating from him.

[story 2]

"So, that's it?" Sarah whispered, the sound barely audible over the muffled chatter of the other hopefuls. Her gaze kept flicking back to the official list, her eyes wide and unfocused. She felt a sudden, alarming lightness in her chest, a sensation that made her breath catch. She clenched her fists until her knuckles were white, the tension radiating up her arms. When the coach finally called the final names, Sarah’s vision tunneled. The words that were spoken seemed distant, muffled, as if she were underwater. A strange, buzzing sensation started behind her eyes, and she squeezed her eyes shut, willing the noise to stop, anything to escape the sudden, overwhelming pressure building inside her chest.

[story 3]

I walked into the gymnasium, the squeak of my sneakers on the polished floor sounding deafeningly loud in the sudden silence. I scanned the seating arrangement, my gaze snagging on the names of the selected players. My mouth went dry; I could taste metal. Each name that wasn't mine was a sharp, unwelcome intrusion. My legs felt strangely unsteady, like they were filled with water. I tried to walk toward the bench, but my feet seemed rooted to the spot, heavy and unresponsive. A deep, unsettling chill washed over me, starting at my toes and creeping up, making the fine hairs on my arms stand on end. I just stood there, a statue of disbelief, unable to move, the world tilting slightly off its axis.
